summary: Provision LoanFlow 360 Core AWS Infrastructure with Terraform
description:
Implement all LoanFlow 360 core AWS infrastructure as Terraform code under infra/terraform/, structured with reusable modules and per-environment root configurations for dev, test, and prod. The module library covers networking (VPC, subnets, security groups), ECR repositories, IAM roles, ECS cluster, RDS MySQL, ElastiCache Redis, MSK Kafka, S3, and DocumentDB. Each environment directory under infra/terraform/environments/ consumes these modules with environment-specific variable overrides — dev and test use smaller instance sizes and relaxed availability settings; prod enables Multi-AZ, deletion protection, and stricter retention. Remote state is stored in S3 with DynamoDB locking, bootstrapped once before any environment is applied. No infrastructure is created by hand — every resource is reproducible from a terraform apply.

Directory layout:
```
infra/
└── terraform/
    ├── bootstrap/
    ├── modules/
    │   ├── networking/
    │   ├── ecr/
    │   ├── iam/
    │   ├── ecs/
    │   ├── rds/
    │   ├── elasticache/
    │   ├── msk/
    │   ├── s3/
    │   └── documentdb/
    └── environments/
        ├── dev/
        ├── test/
        └── prod/
```
functionality: define all LoanFlow 360 AWS infrastructure as reusable Terraform modules consumed by per-environment root configurations so that any environment can be created, updated, or destroyed with a single terraform apply or terraform destroy
benefit: eliminate manual AWS console provisioning, enforce environment parity through shared modules with environment-specific overrides, and produce a version-controlled, reviewable, and repeatable infrastructure definition that can be audited and reproduced on demand

stories.summary: Bootstrap Terraform remote state backend with S3 and DynamoDB
stories.description: Provision the S3 bucket and DynamoDB table used to store Terraform remote state and enforce state locking before any environment is applied. The bootstrap configuration lives in infra/terraform/bootstrap/ and is applied once manually by the AWS system administrator using local state. After bootstrapping, every environment backend.tf file references the resulting S3 bucket and DynamoDB table. A separate state prefix is used per environment (dev/, test/, prod/) so environment state files are isolated. The S3 bucket has versioning and SSE-S3 encryption enabled and all public access blocked.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: S3 state bucket exists with versioning and encryption enabled
acceptanceCriteria.given: the bootstrap configuration has been applied
acceptanceCriteria.when: aws s3api get-bucket-versioning and get-bucket-encryption are run against the state bucket
acceptanceCriteria.then: versioning status is Enabled and server-side encryption is configured with SSE-S3

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: DynamoDB lock table prevents concurrent state modifications
acceptanceCriteria.given: two terraform apply processes target the same environment simultaneously
acceptanceCriteria.when: the second process attempts to acquire the state lock
acceptanceCriteria.then: it receives a lock error and waits or exits rather than corrupting shared state

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Each environment backend references an isolated state key prefix
acceptanceCriteria.given: backend.tf files are configured for dev, test, and prod
acceptanceCriteria.when: terraform init is run in each environment directory
acceptanceCriteria.then: each environment initialises with a distinct state key (dev/terraform.tfstate, test/terraform.tfstate, prod/terraform.tfstate) in the same S3 bucket

tasks.summary: Write infra/terraform/bootstrap/main.tf to provision the S3 state bucket
tasks.description: Create <infra/terraform/bootstrap/main.tf> with an aws_s3_bucket resource named loanflow-terraform-state. Add aws_s3_bucket_versioning with status Enabled, aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration with SSE-S3, and aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block with all four block flags set to true. Add a bucket policy denying s3:PutObject if the request does not use server-side encryption. Use local state for this bootstrap apply — do not reference a remote backend in this directory.

tasks.summary: Write infra/terraform/bootstrap/main.tf DynamoDB lock table resource
tasks.description: Add an aws_dynamodb_table resource to the bootstrap configuration named loanflow-terraform-locks with billing_mode PAY_PER_REQUEST and hash_key LockID of type S. This table is used by all environment backends for state locking. Tag the resource with Project=loanflow-360 and ManagedBy=terraform.

tasks.summary: Write backend.tf for each environment directory referencing the bootstrap resources
tasks.description:
Create <infra/terraform/environments/dev/backend.tf>, <infra/terraform/environments/test/backend.tf>, and <infra/terraform/environments/prod/backend.tf>. Each file configures the s3 backend with:
```
backend "s3" {
  bucket         = "loanflow-terraform-state"
  key            = "{env}/terraform.tfstate"
  region         = var.aws_region
  dynamodb_table = "loanflow-terraform-locks"
  encrypt        = true
}
```
The key prefix differs per environment. Run terraform init in each directory to confirm the backend is reachable and the state file path is correct.

stories.summary: Implement networking Terraform module (VPC, subnets, gateways, security groups)
stories.description: Write the reusable Terraform module at infra/terraform/modules/networking/ that provisions the complete VPC network foundation for any LoanFlow 360 environment. The module accepts variables for CIDR blocks, availability zones, environment name, and tags. It outputs the VPC ID, public and private subnet IDs, and all security group IDs so downstream modules can reference them without hardcoding. Security groups are defined within this module for the ALB, ECS tasks, RDS, ElastiCache, MSK, and DocumentDB tiers.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Module creates a VPC with public and private subnets across two AZs
acceptanceCriteria.given: the networking module is applied with two AZ values
acceptanceCriteria.then: two public subnets and two private subnets exist in the VPC, each in a different availability zone, and the VPC has DNS hostnames and resolution enabled

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Module outputs all security group IDs consumed by downstream modules
acceptanceCriteria.given: the networking module has been applied
acceptanceCriteria.when: module.networking.ecs_sg_id is referenced in a downstream module
acceptanceCriteria.then: the value resolves to the correct security group ID without the consuming module creating its own security group

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Private subnets route outbound traffic through the NAT gateway
acceptanceCriteria.given: the module is applied with nat_gateway_enabled = true
acceptanceCriteria.when: a resource in a private subnet makes an outbound request
acceptanceCriteria.then: the route table for the private subnet has a 0.0.0.0/0 route pointing to the NAT gateway

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Module is reusable across all three environments without code changes
acceptanceCriteria.given: the networking module is referenced in dev, test, and prod environment main.tf files
acceptanceCriteria.then: each environment gets its own isolated VPC with non-overlapping CIDR blocks passed as variables

tasks.description:
Define the following input variables in <infra/terraform/modules/networking/variables.tf>:
- env (string): environment name used in resource naming and tags
- vpc_cidr (string): CIDR block for the VPC (e.g. 10.0.0.0/16 for dev, 10.1.0.0/16 for test, 10.2.0.0/16 for prod)
- public_subnet_cidrs (list of string): two CIDR blocks for public subnets
- private_subnet_cidrs (list of string): two CIDR blocks for private subnets
- availability_zones (list of string): two AZ names in the deployment region
- tags (map of string): additional tags merged onto all resources

tasks.summary: Write VPC, subnet, IGW, NAT gateway, and route table resources in main.tf
tasks.description:
Create <infra/terraform/modules/networking/main.tf> with the following resources:
- aws_vpc with enable_dns_hostnames and enable_dns_support true
- aws_subnet (×4): two public, two private, each with map_public_ip_on_launch false for private
- aws_internet_gateway attached to the VPC
- aws_eip for the NAT gateway
- aws_nat_gateway in the first public subnet
- aws_route_table (×2): public with IGW route, private with NAT route
- aws_route_table_association (×4): each subnet to its route table
Name all resources using the pattern loanflow-${var.env}-{resource-type}.

tasks.summary: Write security group resources for all six resource tiers in main.tf
tasks.description:
Add the following aws_security_group resources to the networking module:
- loanflow-${var.env}-alb-sg: ingress 80 and 443 from 0.0.0.0/0
- loanflow-${var.env}-ecs-sg: ingress on service ports (8080-8085) from alb_sg only
- loanflow-${var.env}-rds-sg: ingress 3306 from ecs_sg only
- loanflow-${var.env}-redis-sg: ingress 6379 from ecs_sg only
- loanflow-${var.env}-msk-sg: ingress 9092 and 9096 from ecs_sg only
- loanflow-${var.env}-docdb-sg: ingress 27017 from ecs_sg only
All groups allow all egress. Use aws_security_group_rule resources for ingress rules referencing source_security_group_id to avoid circular dependency between groups.

Define the following outputs in <infra/terraform/modules/networking/outputs.tf>: vpc_id, public_subnet_ids (list), private_subnet_ids (list), alb_sg_id, ecs_sg_id, rds_sg_id, redis_sg_id, msk_sg_id, docdb_sg_id. These outputs are referenced by all downstream modules using module.networking.<output_name> so no resource IDs are hardcoded in environment configurations.

stories.summary: Implement ECR, IAM, and ECS cluster Terraform modules
stories.description: Write three reusable modules: infra/terraform/modules/ecr/ for ECR repository provisioning with lifecycle policies, infra/terraform/modules/iam/ for the Jenkins EC2 role and ECS task execution role, and infra/terraform/modules/ecs/ for the ECS cluster and Fargate capacity provider. These modules have no external AWS dependencies on each other and can be applied in any order after the networking module exists. All three are consumed identically across dev, test, and prod — they do not have environment-specific sizing differences.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: ECR module creates one repository per service with immutable tags and lifecycle policy
acceptanceCriteria.given: the ECR module is applied with the six service names
acceptanceCriteria.then: six repositories exist under loanflow/ with imageTagMutability IMMUTABLE and a lifecycle policy expiring untagged images after 7 days

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: IAM module outputs the Jenkins instance profile ARN and ECS execution role ARN
acceptanceCriteria.then: both values resolve to valid ARNs that exist in IAM

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: ECS module creates a cluster with Container Insights enabled
acceptanceCriteria.when: aws ecs describe-clusters is run for the created cluster
acceptanceCriteria.then: the cluster is ACTIVE and the containerInsights setting is enabled

tasks.summary: Write infra/terraform/modules/ecr/main.tf for all six service repositories
tasks.description: Create <infra/terraform/modules/ecr/main.tf> with a for_each aws_ecr_repository resource iterating over a variable service_names (list of string defaulting to ["api-gateway", "identity-service", "loan-service", "document-service", "underwriting-service", "notification-service"]). Set image_tag_mutability to IMMUTABLE and enable image scanning on push. Attach an aws_ecr_lifecycle_policy to each repository with a rule expiring untagged images after 7 days and retaining the most recent 20 tagged images.

tasks.summary: Write infra/terraform/modules/iam/main.tf for Jenkins and ECS execution roles
tasks.description:
Create <infra/terraform/modules/iam/main.tf> with:
- aws_iam_role loanflow_jenkins_role with EC2 assume role policy and an inline policy granting ECR push actions on loanflow/* repositories and ECS update-service and describe-services on loanflow-* clusters
- aws_iam_instance_profile loanflow_jenkins_profile wrapping the Jenkins role
- aws_iam_role loanflow_ecs_execution_role with ecs-tasks.amazonaws.com assume role policy
- aws_iam_role_policy_attachment attaching the AmazonECSTaskExecutionRolePolicy managed policy
- aws_iam_role_policy inline policy on the execution role granting secretsmanager:GetSecretValue on arn:aws:secretsmanager:*:*:secret:/loanflow/* and ssm:GetParameters on arn:aws:ssm:*:*:parameter/loanflow/*

tasks.summary: Write infra/terraform/modules/ecs/main.tf for cluster and capacity provider
tasks.description:
Create <infra/terraform/modules/ecs/main.tf> with:
- aws_ecs_cluster named loanflow-${var.env} with a setting block enabling containerInsights
- aws_ecs_cluster_capacity_providers associating FARGATE and FARGATE_SPOT providers with the cluster
- A default_capacity_provider_strategy variable allowing the environment config to set weight 1 on FARGATE_SPOT for dev/test and weight 1 on FARGATE for prod
Output the cluster name and cluster ARN for use in ECS service and task definition resources.

stories.summary: Implement Terraform modules for all managed data stores
stories.description: Write five reusable Terraform modules covering the managed backing services required by LoanFlow 360: infra/terraform/modules/rds/ for MySQL instances, infra/terraform/modules/elasticache/ for the Redis replication group, infra/terraform/modules/msk/ for the Kafka cluster, infra/terraform/modules/s3/ for the document bucket, and infra/terraform/modules/documentdb/ for the MongoDB-compatible cluster. Each module accepts environment-level variables for instance sizing so the same module code serves dev, test, and prod with different tfvars. Each module also creates the corresponding Secrets Manager secret and Parameter Store entries for its connection details.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: RDS module creates one instance per service with the correct subnet group and security group
acceptanceCriteria.given: the RDS module is applied for loan-service in the dev environment
acceptanceCriteria.then: the instance is in a private subnet group, attached to the rds security group, and not publicly accessible

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: MSK module creates the cluster and outputs the bootstrap broker string
acceptanceCriteria.then: the value is a non-empty comma-separated broker string that can be used as a Kafka bootstrap-servers configuration value

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Each data store module writes its connection endpoint to Parameter Store
acceptanceCriteria.given: a data store module is applied
acceptanceCriteria.then: the parameter exists and its value is the endpoint hostname of the provisioned resource

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Module instance size variables produce different resource sizes across environments
acceptanceCriteria.given: dev tfvars sets rds_instance_class = db.t3.micro and prod tfvars sets db.t3.small
acceptanceCriteria.then: the dev RDS instance reports db.t3.micro and the prod instance reports db.t3.small

tasks.summary: Write infra/terraform/modules/rds/main.tf for per-service MySQL instances
tasks.description: Create <infra/terraform/modules/rds/main.tf> accepting variables: env, service_name, instance_class, allocated_storage, multi_az (bool), subnet_ids, security_group_id, deletion_protection (bool). Provision aws_db_subnet_group, aws_db_instance with engine mysql, engine_version 8.0, publicly_accessible false, backup_retention_period 7, and deletion_protection from variable. Generate the master password using a random_password resource and store it in aws_secretsmanager_secret at /loanflow/${var.env}/${var.service_name}/db-password. Write the endpoint hostname to aws_ssm_parameter at /loanflow/${var.env}/${var.service_name}/db-host.

Create <infra/terraform/modules/elasticache/main.tf> accepting variables: env, node_type, num_cache_clusters, subnet_ids, security_group_id. Provision aws_elasticache_subnet_group and aws_elasticache_replication_group with engine redis, engine_version 7.x, at_rest_encryption_enabled true, transit_encryption_enabled true. Generate the auth token using random_password and store in aws_secretsmanager_secret at /loanflow/${var.env}/redis-auth-token. Write the primary endpoint address to aws_ssm_parameter at /loanflow/${var.env}/redis-host.

tasks.summary: Write infra/terraform/modules/msk/main.tf for the Kafka cluster
tasks.description: Create <infra/terraform/modules/msk/main.tf> accepting variables: env, kafka_version, instance_type, number_of_broker_nodes, subnet_ids, security_group_id. Provision aws_msk_cluster with client_authentication sasl scram enabled, encryption_info with in_cluster and in_transit TLS. Create a random_password for the SASL user and store as a JSON string in aws_secretsmanager_secret at /loanflow/${var.env}/msk-sasl-credentials. Use aws_msk_scram_secret_association to associate the secret with the cluster. Write the bootstrap brokers TLS string to aws_ssm_parameter at /loanflow/${var.env}/msk-bootstrap-servers.

Create <infra/terraform/modules/s3/main.tf> with aws_s3_bucket named loanflow-${var.env}-documents, aws_s3_bucket_versioning enabled, aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration with SSE-S3, aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block all true, and an aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration rule transitioning objects older than 90 days to STANDARD_IA. Write the bucket name to aws_ssm_parameter at /loanflow/${var.env}/s3-documents-bucket.

Create <infra/terraform/modules/documentdb/main.tf> accepting variables: env, instance_class, instance_count, subnet_ids, security_group_id, deletion_protection. Provision aws_docdb_subnet_group and aws_docdb_cluster with tls enabled, backup_retention_period 7, and deletion_protection from variable. Generate a random_password and store in aws_secretsmanager_secret at /loanflow/${var.env}/notification-service/docdb-password. Write the endpoint to aws_ssm_parameter at /loanflow/${var.env}/docdb-host.

stories.summary: Write environment root configurations for dev, test, and prod
stories.description: Write the root Terraform configuration for each environment under infra/terraform/environments/{env}/. Each environment directory contains a main.tf that calls all modules with environment-appropriate variable values, a variables.tf, an outputs.tf, a backend.tf, and a terraform.tfvars file that captures all environment-specific overrides. Dev uses the smallest available instance sizes, single-AZ data stores, and no deletion protection. Test mirrors dev sizing but uses a fully isolated VPC CIDR and state prefix. Prod uses production instance sizes, Multi-AZ RDS, deletion protection on all stateful resources, and the FARGATE capacity provider rather than FARGATE_SPOT. A Makefile in infra/terraform/ provides shorthand targets for init, plan, apply, and destroy per environment.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: terraform plan for dev produces no errors and creates the expected resource count
acceptanceCriteria.given: the dev environment directory is initialised with the remote backend
acceptanceCriteria.when: terraform plan is run in infra/terraform/environments/dev
acceptanceCriteria.then: the plan exits with no errors and the resource count matches the expected number of VPC, ECR, IAM, ECS, RDS, ElastiCache, MSK, S3, and DocumentDB resources

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Dev, test, and prod state files are isolated in separate S3 prefixes
acceptanceCriteria.given: all three environments have been initialised
acceptanceCriteria.when: the loanflow-terraform-state S3 bucket is listed
acceptanceCriteria.then: three separate state files exist at dev/terraform.tfstate, test/terraform.tfstate, and prod/terraform.tfstate with no overlap

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Prod environment has deletion protection enabled on RDS and DocumentDB
acceptanceCriteria.given: the prod terraform.tfvars sets deletion_protection = true
acceptanceCriteria.when: terraform apply is run for prod and a terraform destroy is subsequently attempted
acceptanceCriteria.then: the destroy fails with a deletion protection error for RDS and DocumentDB resources, protecting prod data from accidental teardown

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: make plan ENV=dev runs terraform plan in the correct environment directory
acceptanceCriteria.given: the Makefile is present in infra/terraform
acceptanceCriteria.when: make plan ENV=dev is run from the infra/terraform directory
acceptanceCriteria.then: the command executes terraform -chdir=environments/dev plan and outputs the plan without requiring manual directory navigation

tasks.description:
Create <infra/terraform/environments/dev/main.tf> with a terraform block specifying the required_providers (aws ~> 5.0, random ~> 3.0) and the provider block. Call each module using relative source paths:
```
module "networking" {
  source = "../../modules/networking"
  env    = var.env
  ...
}
```
Repeat for ecr, iam, ecs, rds (once per service using for_each or multiple module calls), elasticache, msk, s3, and documentdb. Pass all module.networking outputs (vpc_id, subnet IDs, security group IDs) as inputs to data store and ECS modules.

tasks.summary: Write terraform.tfvars for dev, test, and prod with environment-specific values
tasks.description:
Create <infra/terraform/environments/dev/terraform.tfvars> with:
```
env                  = "dev"
aws_region           = "us-east-1"
vpc_cidr             = "10.0.0.0/16"
rds_instance_class   = "db.t3.micro"
multi_az             = false
deletion_protection  = false
elasticache_node_type = "cache.t3.micro"
msk_instance_type    = "kafka.t3.small"
docdb_instance_class = "db.t3.medium"
```
Create equivalent files for test (vpc_cidr 10.1.0.0/16, same sizing as dev) and prod:
```
env                  = "prod"
vpc_cidr             = "10.2.0.0/16"
rds_instance_class   = "db.t3.small"
multi_az             = true
deletion_protection  = true
elasticache_node_type = "cache.t3.small"
msk_instance_type    = "kafka.m5.large"
docdb_instance_class = "db.r5.large"
```

Duplicate the dev main.tf structure for <infra/terraform/environments/test/main.tf> and <infra/terraform/environments/prod/main.tf>. The module calls are identical — only the variable values in terraform.tfvars differ. For prod, set the ECS module default_capacity_provider_strategy to use FARGATE with weight 1 and no FARGATE_SPOT, matching the production reliability requirement. For test, set the ECS module to use FARGATE_SPOT exclusively to minimise cost for CI integration test runs.

tasks.summary: Write infra/terraform/Makefile with init, plan, apply, destroy, and output targets
tasks.description:
Create <infra/terraform/Makefile> with the following targets, all parameterised by ENV:
```
init:
	terraform -chdir=environments/$(ENV) init

plan:
	terraform -chdir=environments/$(ENV) plan

apply:
	terraform -chdir=environments/$(ENV) apply

destroy:
	terraform -chdir=environments/$(ENV) destroy

output:
	terraform -chdir=environments/$(ENV) output
```
Add a validate target running terraform -chdir=environments/$(ENV) validate and a fmt-check target running terraform fmt -check -recursive to enforce formatting in CI. Document usage in <infra/terraform/README.md>: make init ENV=dev, make plan ENV=prod.

tasks.summary: Write infra/terraform/environments/dev/outputs.tf and validate full plan for all three environments
tasks.description: Create <infra/terraform/environments/dev/outputs.tf> (and equivalent for test and prod) exposing the values most commonly needed by the Jenkins pipeline and ECS configuration: vpc_id, private_subnet_ids, ecs_cluster_name, ecr_registry_url, alb_dns_name, and the ARNs for the Jenkins instance profile and ECS execution role. Run make plan ENV=dev, make plan ENV=test, and make plan ENV=prod and confirm all three plans exit successfully with no errors. Commit the plan output summaries to <infra/terraform/environments/{env}/plan-summary.txt> as a baseline reference.
